yesterdayAnalysis: Yesterday delivered a solid recovery day per plan with an easy 1.7mi pool swim (TSS 21, HR 106) and easy indoor bike (TSS 42, HR 119, 24.9mi). Heart rates were appropriately low for easy efforts. However, the schedule called for Wednesday to be swim + 3mi easy run, and no run was logged — this shifts some volume forward.
todayRecommendation: Execute today's key session: 45min bike with 3x8min intervals at race NP 145-152w. This is your final race-pace bike stimulus before Oregon taper begins — hit the power targets precisely, stay relaxed, and treat it as a race rehearsal. Fresh legs from Monday's full rest should support good execution.
weekProgress: Week 11 of 13 sits at 187/340 TSS (55%) with 3 days remaining including today. The back-loaded schedule means this is on-track, BUT Wednesday's planned 3mi run was not completed. Today's race-pace bike session (likely ~50 TSS) and Friday's 4x200 swim will add significant load, keeping the week viable if Saturday's easy run and Sunday's spin are completed.
keyInsight: Today's 3x8min race-effort bike workout is the last hard session before Oregon — execution quality matters more than adding volume, especially with the missed Wednesday run.
